summary: Frontend-only iteration 4 testing (SEO + expandable trustee cards). 8 of 9 pages pass all SEO assertions (unique document.title, canonical URL, OG/Twitter meta, theme-color, og:locale=en_NG). All 5 trustee cards render; expand/collapse works correctly for trustee-0/2/4 (including Comrade Ifeoma's 5-paragraph bioFull), cards operate independently, chevron rotates, aria-expanded flips, Read more ↔ Show less toggles. Client-side navigation updates document.title and canonical. Mobile viewport (390x844) also works. No console/page errors. ONE CRITICAL BUG: /events/:slug (EventDetail) page does NOT render its <Seo> component — it is imported but never placed in the JSX. As a result title, canonical, og:type=article, and event-specific og:image are all missing on every event detail route.

ui_bugs.issue: Seo component is imported on line 12 but never rendered in the JSX tree. Every /events/:slug route therefore falls back to the default public/index.html <title> ('CASNAGGI — Care Support…') and has NO canonical link, NO og:type, NO og:title, NO og:image, NO og:locale, NO twitter:card. Verified on /events/cash-gifts-nobis-hall-jalingo: document.title stayed on the site default and only 1 meta[name=description] existed (vs. 2 on working pages). This directly violates the iteration-4 requirement: ‘/events/cash-gifts-nobis-hall-jalingo → Cash Gifts for Vulnerable Families & Orphans · CASNAGGI with og:type=article and og:image = event image’.

action_items: In /app/frontend/src/pages/EventDetail.jsx, render <Seo /> inside the returned JSX (right after `return (<>`). Suggested props: title={event.title}, description={event.summary || event.subtitle || event.excerpt}, path={`/events/${event.slug}`}, image={event.image}, type="article". This single addition will fix the title, canonical, og:type=article and event-specific og:image.
critical_code_review_comments: EventDetail.jsx imports Seo but never uses it — dead import indicates the feature was forgotten, not a rendering bug.

index.html still contains a hard-coded meta[name=description]; Helmet appends a second one on most pages so document has duplicate description tags. Not a functional bug (crawlers read the last one) but worth removing the static one from public/index.html to keep the DOM clean.
TrusteeCard.jsx truncation uses a simple 220-char slice on the first paragraph; acceptable for MVP but will break mid-word without word boundary. Minor, not blocking.

success_rate.frontend: 95% (17/18 checks: 8/9 SEO pages + 3/3 trustee toggles + independence + collapse + 5-paragraph bioFull + client-side nav title update + mobile; 1 failing page = EventDetail SEO)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Only EventDetail SEO needs to be re-verified after fix. Expected: page.title() === 'Cash Gifts for Vulnerable Families & Orphans · CASNAGGI', og:type === 'article', og:image should be the event.image (not the default pexels). Everything else already passed: all other page titles, canonical URLs, OG/Twitter meta, theme-color=#E05A47, og:locale=en_NG, all 5 trustee cards expand/collapse independently with correct chevron rotation and aria-expanded, Comrade Ifeoma (trustee-0) shows 5 paragraphs when expanded, client-side nav updates title on Programs/Contact, mobile viewport works. No console errors seen on any route.
rca_of_the_issue: EventDetail.jsx was edited to add `import Seo from '../components/Seo'` but the JSX consumer was never added. The component therefore silently does nothing — React does not warn on unused imports at runtime. Reproduction: navigate to https://charity-redesign-2.preview.emergentagent.com/events/cash-gifts-nobis-hall-jalingo and inspect `document.title` (returns the index.html default) and `document.querySelector('meta[property="og:type"]')` (returns null). Mitigation: place <Seo title={event.title} description={event.summary} path={`/events/${event.slug}`} image={event.image} type="article" /> as the first child of the outer fragment in EventDetail's return statement.
